ch: A:你要去哪裡?
en: A: Where are you going?

ch: B:我要去菜園裡種菜。
en: B: I’m going to plant vegetables in the garden.

ch: A:你在做什麼?
en: A: What are you doing?

ab: matumuhlua sʉsʉngʉ, umasasasapa aku, pukakua sahlumu cucuvanana.
ch: B:草太多了，我在砍草，等一下要澆水。
en: B:There’s too much grass, I’m cutting it, I’ll have to water it later.

ab: talʉlʉvani aku umala kakamalʉ hla talimau a’ʉ i ia?
ch: A:幫我把鐮刀和鋤頭拿來好嗎?
en: A: Will you get my scythes and hoes, please?

ch: B:你放在哪裡呢?
en: B: Where did you put it?

ch: A:放在犁耙旁邊。
en: A: Put it next to the plow rake.

ch: A:你翻過土了嗎?
en: A: Did you turn over the soil?

ab: hlimarikava cu aku kicuvanana, aʉrapa cucuvanana.
ch: B:我剛翻過土，等等要播種。
en: B: I just turned over the soil, and so on to sow seeds.

ab: hlilimikiu u hlatʉngʉ acahli cu i maripucu?
ch: A:你種的菜可以收成了嗎?
en: A: Are your vegetables ready to be harvested?

ab: hlatʉngʉ maipaputu isa naani acahli cu maripucu, hlatʉngʉ pipahlu isa naana kutuahlʉ mana maripucu.
ch: B:這裡的高麗菜可以收成了，那裡的昭和草就還不能收成。
en: B: The cabbage here is ready to be harvested, but the Showa grass there is not yet ready to be harvested.

ab: arʉʉ! tamuucu kitumuhlu cu u mairangʉ.
ch: A:哇!你挖了這麼多的地瓜。
en: A: Wow! You dug up so many gourds.

ab: talʉlʉvani aku mikua tahliulu a’ʉ i ia?
ch: B:幫我放穀倉好嗎?
en: B: Will you help me with the barn?

ch: A:你們在做什麼?
en: A: What are you doing?

ch: B:我們在灑網抓魚。
en: B: We’re setting nets to catch fish. (Sprinkling nets)

ch: A:這裡的魚多嗎?
en: A: Are there many fish here?

ab: rʉrʉmanʉ talahlʉ na apatisangalicu matumuhlu vutukuhlu.
ch: B:魚簍裡已經抓了很多魚。
en: B: The fish basket has been full of fish.

ch: A:你平常怎麼抓魚?
en: A: How do you usually catch fish?

ch: B:我都用魚叉射魚。
en: B: I shoot fish with a spear.

ab: macahlia u i marumia vuuru hla ripasʉ mʉrʉcʉka?
ch: A:你會用弓箭打獵嗎?
en: A: Do you know how to hunt with a bow and arrow?

ab: ma kaupaupangʉ ari na marumia ku ringai maruringai kuli’i, apahlapa marumia mʉmʉa mahlava hlahlitu hla ruavici tasau na mʉrʉcʉka.
ch: B:我通常都是用陷阱抓獵物，偶爾也會拿槍帶狗狩獵。
en: B: I usually catch my prey with a trap, but occasionally I hunt with a gun and a dog.

ch: A:這是山豬所走的路。
en: A: This is the road the boar took.

ch: B:你怎麼知道呢?
en: B: How do you know?

ab: kani’i ia hlikiramia alʉmʉhlʉ, kuhlaisai kani’i ia tatahlʉ alʉmʉhlʉ.
ch: A:這是山豬的腳印，所以這是山豬所走的路。
en: A: These are the footprints of the boar, so this is the path the boar took.

ab: pasakulai musa kumita ringaiita, ausi maru cu a hlimuringai.
ch: A:快去看我們的陷阱，好像抓到獵物了。
en: A: Go and see our trap, for it seems that we have caught our prey.

ch: B:你怎麼知道呢?
en: B: How do you know?

ab: ausi hliumavali cu a tasau na, pasakulaiutai micʉcʉngʉhlʉ.
ch: A:狗好像聞到味道了。我們快追。
en: A: The dog seems to have smelled it. Let’s follow them. (Chase)

ch: A:你正在製作的是什麼東西?
en: A: What are you working on?

ch: B:這是石壓式陷阱，用來抓老鼠的。
en: B: This is a stone trap, used to catch rats.

ch: A:你放在那裡的是什麼東西?
en: A: What is that thing you put there?

ab: kana’a ia silipi, maruamiamu isana matisangalʉ taurungu.
ch: B:那是套腳式繩索陷阱，我們用它來抓山羌。
en: B: It was a leg-rigged rope trap, and we used it to catch Shankiang.

ab: sia’ʉ’ʉraisamana ’ihliculangica na uvura siarasingasinga, arinani hlikuritumuhluamu kuli’i.
ch: A:感謝祖靈賜福，今天我們有很多獵物收獲。
en: A: Thanks to the blessing of our ancestors, we had a great harvest today.

ch: B:是啊!我們帶回部落好好分享吧!
en: B: Yeah! Let’s take it back to the tribe and share it!
